title: Tools, Resources, Prompts, and Server Instructions · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Design a minimal MCP surface for an internal knowledge and action service. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an MCP primitive and naming contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Expose actions as narrow tools with descriptive schemas and side-effect annotations.
Expose readable addressable context as resources or templates rather than action tools.
Expose reusable user-controlled prompt templates only when they improve discovery and composition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Tool Contracts · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: agent ext mcp primitives. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: agent ext mcp primitives. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Tool Contracts without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for expose actions as narrow tools with descriptive schemas and side-effect annotations.

content.text: Expose actions as narrow tools with descriptive schemas and side-effect annotations. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext mcp primitives. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Tool Contracts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an MCP primitive and naming contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One generic execute tool is the most flexible MCP design. This fails the Tool Contracts contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: OpenAI Codex Manual: Build Skills (Verified current 2026-07-13) — Controls Codex skill structure, progressive disclosure, locations, invocation, plugins, and dependencies.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose actions as narrow tools with descriptive schemas and side-effect annotations.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Primitive Choice applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose the smallest primitive that preserves consent, discoverability, caching, and semantics.
